title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: People say that the old mansion is haunted by a ghost.
questions.explanation: Tests the passive reporting structure (Subject + is said + to-infinitive).
questions.secondSentence: The old mansion (1) _________ haunted by a ghost.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I’ll definitely call you tomorrow,’ Sarah said to me.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with infinitive and direct/indirect objects.
questions.secondSentence: Sarah (1) _________ a call the following day.

questions.firstSentence: If I hadn't missed the train, I wouldn't be late for the meeting.
questions.explanation: Tests mixed conditionals and inverted conditional structures.
questions.secondSentence: I wouldn't be late for the meeting (1) _________ the train.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure he didn't mean to offend you with that comment.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative past deduction.
questions.secondSentence: He (1) _________ to offend you with that comment.

questions.firstSentence: He had hardly finished his speech when the audience started cheering.
questions.secondSentence: No (1) _________ his speech than the audience started cheering.

questions.firstSentence: It is much more difficult to learn Chinese than I expected.
questions.explanation: Tests negative comparison using 'not nearly as... as'.
questions.secondSentence: Learning Chinese is (1) _________ as I expected.
